rationale: Consensus No. 1 and the franchise wing scorer who fits cleanly between Sarr's rim protection and Trae Young's playmaking.
Consensus No. 2 is also their stated top need, a lead initiator and No. 1 perimeter shot creator.
Best player available for a full rebuild, an elite-production cornerstone scorer with the class's safest profile.
BPA whose two-way athleticism gives the Giddey-Buzelis core a defensive backbone; the center need waits for pick 15.
Fit-driven divergence, every guard here duplicates Garland, so they swing on the 6'10 wing with Tatum-cohort upside as star insurance behind a 35-year-old Kawhi.
Pure BPA as the front office intends, the consensus No. 5 slips one spot and his processing lifts the young guard group.
The teardown's stated top need is a franchise lead guard and Acuff is the best scoring point guard on the board.
Post-Trae creation need met with the class's best pick-and-roll passing flashes, matching their young-upside tendency.
Elite rim-pressure guard as the secondary creator next to Flagg and insurance on Kyrie's age and ACL.
Fit-and-tendency divergence over Burries, with the Giannis decision unset they take the 19-year-old athletic wing for optionality, matching their teen-upside drafting history.
Consensus value that fell two spots, and the ready-made two-way guard fits their older plug-and-play drafting pattern for the Curry window.
The stated backup-center need answered with the best rim protector in the class, plus passing that fits their connective offense.
Lead-guard playmaking need plus asset value for the Giannis pursuit, the best on-ball creator left.
Slight slide stops here, a ready-made center-sized defender of all five positions fits their pivot to plug-and-play skill and their defensive needs.
Fit-driven divergence two spots ahead of median, the rim-protecting defensive big is the loudest remaining need after the pick-4 forward.
Older Big 12 shooting wing with elite vertical athleticism, exactly Memphis's plug-and-play drafting type and their shooting need.
Cheap rookie-scale frontcourt depth, the class's best rebounder for a tax-bound champion drafting for rotation slots.
Fit-driven divergence four spots ahead of median, the starting-center hole goes unfilled at 14 so the young 6'11 big is the sequencing play.
Productive-college tendency meets the shooting and bench-scoring needs, 51.7% catch-and-shoot with the best feel in the class.
A patient contender with upside-teen tendencies absorbs the knee risk for star-level defensive tools as Wembanyama frontcourt insurance.
One player answers two needs for a 60-win team, 41.5% three-point shooting around Cade plus backup playmaking.
Wing size and defense over another guard, the 6'7 two-way wing fills the clearest hole around Maxey, Embiid and Edgecombe.
Value at median 20 that happens to shoot 41% from three with rare defensive instincts, fitting both the shooting need and the young-upside tendency.
A guard-saturated late teens lets the backup-point-guard need meet a player sliding four spots below median, cheap rotation depth for a second-apron Finals team.
Fit-driven divergence, the 22-year-old stretch 7-footer answers the starting-center hole next to Luka and matches their older plug-and-play tendency.
Volume movement shooting for the bench-scoring need, squarely in their older-shooter drafting profile.
Best talent in range, the athletic contact-seeking forward brings the bench rim pressure they lacked, value over a center reach.
The 7'2 teen international is the Gobert succession swing that exactly matches their Beringer-style upside tendency.
Fit-driven pick over pure board order, the college-ready 6'8 versatile defender answers wing depth and point-of-attack defense for a second-apron contender.
A positional run on bigs and forwards in the 20s drops the 41.6% shooter to a team that stated No. 30 suits a ready role player, wing shooting for Flagg.
